Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input An event log from a fictional financial services company containing three cases C001 C002 C003 Problem Some activity names eg Verify Documents Call Customer Initiate Request Approve Request appear multiple times within single cases but represent different specific actions or stages based on context timestamp resource additional context Task Identify recurring activities within cases Interpret the contextual differences Rename the activities to reflect these differences Present the revised log Justify the naming choices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Event Log Case by Case Case C001 Observation Verify Documents happens twice First is initial screening second is re verification after remediation Call Customer happens once here

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C002 Observation Verify Documents happens once Call Customer happens twice First is problem solving discrepancy second is confirmation closure

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C003 Observation Verify Documents happens twice First is initial physical check second is post correction check Call Customer happens once logistics return Initiate Request is different channel Branch vs Web

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Recurring Activities and Contextual Differences Correction I should focus on activities that repeat within a single case ID Recurring within cases Verify Documents Appears twice in C001 and C003 Initial vs Post Remediation Call Customer Appears twice in C002 Discrepancy Resolution vs Confirmation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait lets re read the prompt The same activity names appear multiple times within a single case Okay so I must prioritize activities that repeat within a case

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refinement To make the log cleaner I should also consider if Verify Documents in C002 once should align with the new naming convention established for C001 C003 Initial vs Re Since it only happens once in C002 its likely the Initial

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE

Text: Call Customer C001 Request Missing Docs Call C002 Resolve Account Discrepancy Call C002 Confirm Adjustments Call C003 Branch Recall Call

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: To keep the log readable but distinct I will use suffixes or specific descriptors

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will standardize the naming convention across the log for clarity even if an activity only appears once in a specific case provided it belongs to a category that varies across the log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Revising the Log Verify Documents First time in a case Initial Document Verification Second time in a case Re Verification of Documents Call Customer C001 Request Missing Docs Call C002 first Discrepancy Clarification Call C002 second Adjustment Confirmation Call C003 Branch Recall Call

Text: Initiate Request Web vs Branch Ill add the channel for clarity though it doesnt repeat per case Online Request Initiation vs Branch Request Initiation Adjust Account Keep as Internal Account Adjustment Submit Additional Keep as Customer Document Submission Approve Request Keep as Final Request Approval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints Did I identify activities appearing multiple times within the same case Yes Verify Documents in C001 C003 Call Customer in C002 Did I rename them to reflect context Yes Is there a revised log Yes Is there justification Yes
